content: We can frame the open-ended comparison between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and generalized curious agents as an active learning problem where the agent itself directs which comparative performance metrics to explore, rather than having researchers pre-define all comparison axes. This turns a static benchmarking task into a self-driven discovery process where the agent uncovers unexpected strengths and weaknesses of different curiosity mechanisms that human researchers would not prioritize testing.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
